data.title: Apple removes Bitchat app after China request. App used in protests in five countries. Launched in July.
data.description: Bitchat was introduced in July of the previous year and has been utilized in various protests across Madagascar, Uganda, Nepal, Indonesia, and Iran. Authorities in these countries have made efforts to limit internet access during these incidents.

data.title: Michael Saylor teases next Bitcoin buy strategy after pausing for a week; hints at future purchase on Sunday.
data.description: On Sunday, Michael Saylor made a post indicating a return to work on X, suggesting a possible Bitcoin acquisition. This move comes after the company halted purchases the previous week.

data.title: Trump's ultimatum spikes crypto values; hints at Iran deal nearing as he warns of consequences if Strait of Hormuz not opened.
data.description: The President of the United States, Donald Trump, issued a warning to Iran stating that the country could face dire consequences if it does not comply with opening the Strait of Hormuz. Trump suggested that Iran may be "living in Hell" if they fail to do so. However, in a separate statement to reporters, Trump mentioned that there is potential for a deal with Iran to be reached soon. The remarks come amid escalating tensions between the two countries.

data.title: Liven AS announces intention to conduct an initial public offering of shares and to apply for admission to trading on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange Main List
data.description: Liven AS, known as Liven, plans to go public in the upcoming spring with an initial public offering of shares. The company aims to list its shares on the Nasdaq Tallinn Stock Exchange Main List. The offering will target retail and institutional investors in Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. The capital raised from the share issuance will be allocated towards acquiring new development properties and executing the company's long-term growth strategy. Additionally, the move will assist in fortifying the group's capital structure for future endeavors.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall As Trump Sees Iran Deal Or 'Hell'; Sandisk Leads 7 Stocks To Watch
data.description: The President made a significant threat, warning that Iran could face severe consequences if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. He suggested that the country would suffer greatly if the situation escalates. Meanwhile, efforts are being made to boost the market, but investors should keep an eye out for a key indicator. Following this news, Dow Jones Futures took a hit. Sandisk and six other stocks are highlighted as ones to watch amidst the ongoing tension.

data.title: Federal Industries Introduces Vision Series® Salad Case™, A Refrigerated Merchandiser Engineered for Faster Salad Assembly and Fresher Ingredients
data.description: A new specialized system has been developed to enhance efficiency, increase product quality, and cater to large-scale foodservice needs. Marked by its focus on optimizing operations, freshness, and volume capacities, the innovative solution comes from Federal Industries®. Known for producing top-notch food display and specialized refrigeration equipment, the company unveiled the product on April 6, 2026 in Belleville, Wisconsin.

data.title: LEIFRAS Co., Ltd. Plans to Launch a Dribbling School with Dribble Designer Masakazu Okabe in Spring 2026
data.description: In an effort to provide children all over Japan with opportunities to develop soccer skills, LEIFRAS Co., Ltd. (Nasdaq: LFS) has announced a new partnership. The company, focused on youth sports and community involvement, aims to create an environment conducive to learning. The initiative, launched on April 6, 2026, seeks to positively impact a significant number of young individuals by fostering their athletic abilities and promoting social interaction.
